name: South Sudan
headline: South Sudan FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — IRC May 2026 Emergency Watchlist ranks South Sudan 3rd globally (behind only Sudan and OPT). Akobo State renewed fighting: 200,000 displaced, 100,000 fled to Ethiopia (cross-border displacement event). 73,000 facing starvation and death in Upper Nile/Jonglei. 2.2 million children under 5 acutely malnourished. River aid routes cut off — new access-dimension signal. HRW May 6 'civilians starving in conflict areas' report. UN ERC Fletcher UNSC warning: 'full-scale famine and collapse' risk. 10 million people requiring humanitarian assistance. Last assessed April 18 — 27 days; new material evidence since then including the IRC global rank, Akobo crisis cross-border displacement, HRW primary report, and UN ERC UNSC testimony. Floor sustained at 0.0; no score change; new conduct documentation added.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. IRC Emergency Watchlist rank 3 globally is a comparative-awareness data point — international monitoring is producing high-quality documentation; state response is absent. Awareness profile elsewhere; floor evidence is the response gap.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. 73,000 facing starvation; 2.2 million children acutely malnourished; 10 million needing humanitarian assistance. EMP-dimension protective infrastructure absent.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. State action in Akobo continued fighting; no ceasefire honored; river aid routes cut off as a documented access-restriction event.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Akobo State and Upper Nile/Jonglei populations disproportionately affected; no equity-dimension protective infrastructure.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. IHL violations on civilian protection ongoing; cross-border displacement to Ethiopia (100,000 fled) is a non-refoulement and refugee-protection adjacent event.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. UN ERC Fletcher UNSC testimony is external accountability invocation; no actor self-accountability.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. River aid routes cut off is a systems-level access-restriction signal — humanitarian infrastructure denial. Famine risk at full-scale level per UN ERC.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. No INT-dimension uplift evidence in window.

evidence.summary: IRC May 2026: South Sudan renewed fighting in Akobo State leading to alarming humanitarian crisis. IRC Emergency Watchlist rank 3 globally (behind Sudan and OPT).
HRW May 6: Civilians starving in South Sudan's conflict areas.
IRC: 10 million people need help in South Sudan.
WFP: 'There is no food' — multiple crises push South Sudan deeper into hunger. 2.2 million children under 5 acutely malnourished.

keyEvidence: IRC May 2026 Emergency Watchlist rank 3 globally (behind only Sudan and OPT) — new comparative metric.
Akobo State renewed fighting: 200,000 displaced, 100,000 fled to Ethiopia (cross-border displacement).
73,000 facing starvation and death in Upper Nile/Jonglei.
2.2 million children under 5 acutely malnourished; 10 million needing humanitarian assistance.
River aid routes cut off — new access-dimension signal.
HRW May 6 'civilians starving in conflict areas' primary report.
UN ERC Fletcher UNSC warning: 'full-scale famine and collapse' risk.
Floor sustained at 0.0; no score change; new conduct documentation added.
rationale: Floor confirmation at 0.0. Multiple new material-evidence items since April 18 last assessment: IRC global rank 3 (comparative metric), Akobo crisis with cross-border displacement to Ethiopia, HRW May 6 primary report, UN ERC UNSC testimony, river aid route disruption (new SYS access-restriction signal). All eight dimensions remain at anchor 1. Math: 8/8 = 1.0 → ((1.0-1)/4)*100 = 0.0.
watches: Akobo State fighting and displacement trajectory
Cross-border displacement to Ethiopia — secondary-state burden-sharing
Famine declaration status (currently 'full-scale famine risk' per UN ERC)
River aid route restoration (or further restriction)
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology observation: 'river-aid-route-denial' is a distinct SYS-dimension access-restriction signal in landlocked or river-dependent humanitarian contexts. Different from 'border-closure' (state-controlled) or 'airspace-closure' (combatant-controlled) — river access denial typically requires combatant control over specific waterways, which is a documented IHL violation pattern.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not applicable — floor entity; no boundary risk; no math drift.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. IRC rank 3 and Akobo crisis documented; does not affect composite arithmetic.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
